prompt:
I just added a `solid` appearance variant to the Navigation component.
Did you document this variant in the playground? You almost never do.
Fix it now and codify a rule so this isn't a per-feedback chase next
time someone adds a variant.
response:
Now I have everything. Let me synthesize the exact state:

**Confirmed findings:**
1. The playground page at `playground/src/app/components/navigation/page.tsx` **already has the `solid` variant** — both `ComponentPreview` blocks, both code samples, and the `PropsTable` row are present and correct
2. `Navigation.module.scss` has **no `.solid` class** — the TSX references `styles.solid` but the rule doesn't exist yet (the `solid` appearance renders as functionally identical to `frosted` with no visual difference)
3. The "codify a rule" ask: `EAP-082` exists in the docs layer but there is **no structural step** in `.cursor/rules/playground-components.md` that enforces variant-completeness as part of the variant-addition workflow itself

So there are **two real fixes** needed: (1) the SCSS `.solid` class, and (2) the rule codification. The playground page itself is already complete. Let me now produce all three outputs.

---

## Diagnosis

Three distinct findings, not one:

| # | Finding | File | Severity |
|---|---------|------|----------|
| 1 | **Playground page is already complete** | `playground/src/app/components/navigation/page.tsx` | ✅ No work needed |
| 2 | **SCSS `.solid` class is missing** | `src/components/ui/Navigation/Navigation.module.scss` | 🔴 The variant renders but has zero visual differentiation — `styles.solid` is appended to the class list but the rule doesn't exist in the stylesheet |
| 3 | **Variant-sync has no structural enforcement in the rules layer** | `.cursor/rules/playground-components.md` | 🟡 EAP-082 exists in the docs layer but there's no checklist step that fires during variant addition |

---

## Root Cause

**EAP-082** — *Adding a Variant/Prop to a DS Component Without Updating the Playground Page.* Enforced by **Guardrail Engineering-24** (`ALWAYS update the playground page when adding a new variant, prop, or visual state in the same session`).

The secondary cause is **EAP-008** — *Documenting Recurring Fixes in Docs Instead of Promoting to Rules.* The variant-sync obligation exists at the docs/anti-pattern layer but has never been wired into the rules layer as a blocking pre-completion checklist. Every enforcement of guardrail-24 has been reactive (per-feedback) rather than structural.

---

## Fix

### Fix 1 — Add the `.solid` SCSS class

**`src/components/ui/Navigation/Navigation.module.scss`** — append after `.nav { ... }`:

```scss
.solid {
  background-color: var(--portfolio-surface-terra-05);
  backdrop-filter: none;
  -webkit-backdrop-filter: none;

  // The blur-tail pseudo-element is only meaningful on the frosted variant.
  // Remove it entirely on solid so there's no bleed artifact at the nav edge.
  &::after {
    display: none;
  }
}
```

> **Why:** The frosted default uses `background-color: var(--portfolio-surface-terra-subtle)` which is a semi-transparent surface token. The `.solid` modifier must override to the fully opaque `terra-05` surface and kill the `backdrop-filter` pseudo-element — otherwise it's visually identical to `frosted`. The component already does `styles.solid && styles.solid` class-merging in the TSX; the stylesheet just never defined the rule.

---

### Fix 2 — Add a variant-completeness checklist step to the playground rules

**`.cursor/rules/playground-components.md`** — add a new section after the **Intent Gate** block:

```markdown
## Variant / Prop Completeness Gate (BLOCKING on component edits)

**When you add a new variant, prop, or visual state to any component in `src/components/ui/`**, you MUST complete all of the following before that session ends. This is a pre-completion gate, not a post-hoc review.

**Checklist — run through this for EVERY new variant/prop:**

1. [ ] `ComponentPreview` block added to the playground page for the new variant — shows the live component rendering
2. [ ] Code snippet (`const code…`) added that demonstrates the new prop/value
3. [ ] `PropsTable` row updated — new prop name, type union includes the new value, default is correct, description explains *when to use* (semantic intent), not what CSS changes
4. [ ] Notes section updated if the variant has usage constraints (e.g., "use solid when content doesn't scroll behind nav")
5. [ ] Flush-and-restart completed (per the gate above) and the new section verified in the curl response

**Never defer to "next session."** A variant that exists in `src/` but has no playground documentation is invisible to every future consumer. See **EAP-082**, **Guardrail Engineering-24**.
```

---

### Fix 3 — Log this incident in `docs/engineering-feedback-log.md`

Prepend the following entry to the top of the recent log (after the `> Last updated:` header line):

```markdown
ENG-267: Navigation `solid` appearance variant added to TSX without a corresponding `.solid` SCSS rule — `styles.solid` was appended to `navClass` but `.solid {}` never defined in `Navigation.module.scss`, so both variants rendered identically. Playground page was already complete (ComponentPreview, code sample, PropsTable row all present). Root cause: EAP-082 (variant added without full artifact set); secondary EAP-008 (variant-sync rule lived in docs layer only, not in `.cursor/rules/playground-components.md`). Fix: added `.solid` SCSS class (opaque `terra-05` surface, `backdrop-filter: none`, `::after { display: none }`); added Variant/Prop Completeness Gate checklist to `.cursor/rules/playground-components.md`.
```

---

## Principle

**EAP-082 / Guardrail Engineering-24:** A variant is not shipped until three artifacts exist in the same session — the implementation (SCSS/TSX), the playground `ComponentPreview`, and the `PropsTable` row. Any one missing makes the variant invisible or broken for all future consumers.

The structural lesson from **EAP-008** is sharper: EAP-082 has been cited on at least ENG-106 and now ENG-267 without the feedback loop stopping. That's the signal that a docs-layer anti-pattern needs promotion to the rules layer. The Variant/Prop Completeness Gate added to `.cursor/rules/playground-components.md` (Fix 2 above) creates the blocking checklist that makes this a pre-condition instead of a per-feedback chase — exactly what `docs/engineering.md §8.2` prescribes when the same category recurs.
